ncs_standard_definition.description_short: Botanists study the structure, growth, evolution, classification, and distribution of plants, algae, and fungi. They work in various fields, including research, conservation, and education.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: Botanists conduct experiments, collect and analyze data, and develop new theories and models to understand plant biology. They may work in laboratories, greenhouses, or outdoor environments, and often collaborate with other scientists and experts.
ncs_standard_definition.official_min_education: M.Sc. in Botany or related field

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in botany can start by taking courses in biology, chemistry, and mathematics.

choice_maker.description: Individuals with a generic degree can pursue a postgraduate degree in botany or a related field to transition into this career.

future_builder.description: Experienced botanists can advance to senior roles by developing expertise in specific areas, such as plant breeding, genetics, or conservation.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2-3 years of experience as a Research Assistant or Junior Botanist

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into botany by acquiring relevant skills and experience.
